description_long: This object is a retro-style portable radio receiver, commonly known as a boombox, with a light blue plastic exterior and a robust rectangular form. The front face features two large circular black speaker grilles, a central cassette tape deck, and an array of tactile buttons, dials, and sliders for tuning and audio control. Two telescopic metal antennas extend from the top, flanking a sturdy handle for easy carrying. The sides are vented for heat dissipation, and the back is mostly flat with molded details and small feet for stability. The control panel includes labeled switches, a frequency indicator, and cassette operation buttons, all rendered in white and silver. The overall design is reminiscent of 1980s urban street culture, blending functionality with bold visual appeal.
description: A light blue retro boombox with dual black speakers, a central cassette deck, and silver antennas, featuring tactile controls and a sturdy handle.
description_view_0: The front view shows the dual black speaker grilles, cassette deck, control panel with buttons, dials, and sliders, as well as the extended antennas and handle.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the vented panel for heat dissipation and the depth of the object, with minimal controls visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a flat blue surface with molded details, small feet, and the back of the handle and antennas, with no controls or speakers visible.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the vented panel and the object's thickness, with the edge of the control panel just visible.
